text:
Once upon a time in the quiet town of Meadowleaf, there was a peculiar old lady named Eleanor Mapleleaf. She lived in a charming cottage near a forest clearing that boasted a small pond surrounded by flowering shrubs.

Eleanor had a hobby peculiar to her—she kept pets. Instead of cats or birds, she adored parrots. There was one particularly large yellow bird she had somehow convinced to perch on her shoulder during afternoon tea.

One day, while browsing old books at the town's library, Eleanor stumbled upon an old tome that talked about parrots speaking ancient languages. Fascinated, she spent countless afternoons reading about them. Her fascination grew into a passion, and she collected every book she could find on parrots.

Her cottage became a sanctuary filled with feathered friends—though quite a few were rather hard to calm. One particular parrot named Petunia had an exceptionally large feathered head crest, that she'd sing about every morning.
